MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New DeUil, the 30th November 1970 G.S.R. 1971.โIn exercise of the powers conferred by section 36, read with sub-section (2) of section 32, of the Bihar and Uttar Pradesh (Alteration of Boundaries) Act, 1868 (24 of 1968j. the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely:โ 1. (1) Short title and commencement.โThese rules may be called the Bihar and Uttar Pradesh (Inspection of Boundary Pillars) Rules, 1970. (2) They shall come into force at once. -- 1 of 39 -- 926 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PAST I I - 2. Definitions.โIn these rules, unless the context otherwise requires,โ (1) "Act" means the Bihar and Uttar Pradesh (Alteration of Boundaries) Act, 1068 (24; of 1068); (2) "Boundary pillar" means a boundary pillar constructed or to be constructed under sub-section (1) of section 32 of the Act on a fixed point; (3) "fixed point" means a point on the fixed boundary, determined under clause (b) of sub-section (3) of section 3 of the Act, for the construction of a boundary pillar; (4) "Schedule" means the Schedule appended to these rules showing the coordinates and other particulars relating to the fixed points of the .boundary pillars. 3. Inspection of the boundary pillars.โ(1) As soon as may be alter the mon- soon is over, but before the 30th November each year, the boundary pillars shall be inspected jointly by the officers of the State Governments of Bihar and Uttar Pradesh: Provided that the inspection to be carried out for the first time under the provisions of these 'rules shall be completed before the 31st December, 1970. (2) In the absence of an agreement to the contrary between the two State Governments, the inspection shall be doneโโข (a) by the Collectors of the districts on the periphery of which the boundary pillar lies, or (b) by such Revenue Officers, being officers not below the rank of Deputy Collectors, as may be nominated in this behalf by the Collector concerned. 4. Construction and maintenance of the boundary pillars.โ(l) If during the inspection, any boundary pillar is found not to have been constructed or to be missing, damaged or displaced, it shall be constructed, reconstructed or repaired, as the case may be. (2) If any fixed point is not readily identifiable, it shall be re-located by a oint team of revenue officers of the two Governments nominated by the Col- ectors concerned, such re-location being done on the basis of t h e coordinates and other particulars set out in the Schedule. (3) The cost of constructing reconstructing or repairing any pillar shall be borne by the State Government mentioned^ against that particular pillar in t h e Schedule. (4) Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions Of this rule, the Collectors concerned may agree that any particular boundary pillar need not be construct- ed, re-constructed or repaired if the fixed point of the pillar is under deep water or for any other reason, which may be recorded in writing. 5.
